topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a familiar profile picture, a smug little smile. Mr. Harrison. He was tagging me in a post about a class discussion, a caption dripping with condescension. A slow, cold knot tightened in my stomach, spreading outward like ice. I stared at the screen, my jaw clenching so hard my molars ached. Every carefully constructed academic success I’d built felt suddenly tainted, exposed by this digital leash he’d fastened around me. I didn't reply. Instead, I navigated to his profile, my fingers hovering over the 'block' button, a decision hardening in my chest. I wanted him to see what it felt like to be completely unseen, to have his carefully curated world suddenly become a wasteland of silence.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked away in a forgotten folder on my laptop—screenshots of his recent activity, the undeniable proof of his constant surveillance. A hot flush crept up my neck, quickly morphing into a simmering, dangerous heat. It wasn't just the intrusion; it was the implication that he had cataloged every late night, every whispered doubt. I walked to my desk, the floorboards seeming to vibrate under my sudden, restless energy. My hands balled into fists, the tension radiating up my arms. I took a deep, shaky breath, forcing my expression into a mask of bored indifference, but inside, a sharp, focused determination took root. I began meticulously planning a counter-move, something subtle, something that would make him question the security of his own digital fortress.

[story 3]

The realization hit me like a physical blow during my lunch break. A shared photo from a mandatory school event, captioned by Mr. Harrison, included me prominently featured, looking exactly as I did in real life. The casualness of it was infuriating. A low growl started deep in my chest, a sound I barely recognized. I didn't slam my phone down; instead, I placed it deliberately on the table, the click echoing loudly in the otherwise quiet cafeteria. My gaze swept across the room, lingering on the periphery, searching for anyone who might notice the shift in my posture, the sudden stillness that preceded a storm. I felt a deep, internal churning, a need to disrupt the order he had imposed, to see the façade crack under the weight of my deliberate, quiet opposition.
